mechanism_narrative: The 6-month change in industrial production (INDPRO) is a reliable leading indicator of economic growth; positive changes signal strengthening demand for capital, lifting real yields and pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of INDPRO 6-month change against 20-day forward yield change is less than 0.05 over a rolling 3-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below the natural rate threshold signals labor market tightening, raising expectations of future inflation and monetary policy normalization, which pushes 10-year yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.0% on a monthly basis
failure_condition: If, in instances where UNRATE crosses below 7%, the 20-day forward yield change is negative in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) provides a composite signal of robust consumption and residential investment, reinforcing expectations of sustained GDP growth and pushing Treasury yields higher.
failure_condition: If the composite indicator of RSAFS and HOUST 3-month growth yields a rank IC less than 0.05 against 20-day yield changes, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate policy environment (FEDFUNDS near zero), any uptick in core CPI inflation is magnified by markets as signaling eventual exit from accommodative policy, leading to a sharp rise in long-term Treasury yields.
failure_condition: If during ZIRP periods (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%), the correlation between monthly CPI change and 20-day yield change is not significantly positive (p>0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production surprises markets negatively, triggering a flight-to-quality bid for Treasuries that depresses yields over the next week.
failure_condition: If when INDPRO MoM is negative, the 5-day yield change is positive (yields rise) in more than 40% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The trailing 3-month change in housing starts is a procyclical leading indicator; a decline in HOUST signals softening residential investment, reducing growth expectations and lowering Treasury yields.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between 3-month change in HOUST and 20-day yield changes is above -0.03 (i.e., not negative), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is elevated and industrial production simultaneously contracts, markets revise down growth expectations sharply, leading to a sustained fall in 10-year yields over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If the interaction of high UNRATE (above median) and declining INDPRO does not predict negative 60-day yield changes with a hit rate above 60%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE above 7.5%), negative surprises in nonfarm payrolls reinforce concerns about a stalled recovery, prompting a flight-to-quality that drives 10-year yields lower.
failure_condition: If when UNRATE > 7.5% and PAYEMS MoM is negative, the 20-day yield change is positive in more than half of occurrences, the hypothesis is rejected.
